evals.prompt: I just added Jahro to my Unity project through the Package Manager. What do I do next?
evals.expected_output: Guides user through API key setup at console.jahro.io, pasting key in Tools > Jahro Settings, and opening console with ~ or triple-tap
expectations: Mentions API key setup at console.jahro.io
References Tools > Jahro Settings menu path
Explains how to open console (~ key or triple-tap on mobile)
Does not invent non-existent Jahro APIs

evals.prompt: How can I tell if Jahro is already installed in my Unity project?
evals.expected_output: Suggests checking Packages/manifest.json for io.jahro.console and looking for using JahroConsole imports
expectations: Mentions Packages/manifest.json
References io.jahro.console package identifier
Suggests checking for JahroConsole namespace usage
Does not suggest incorrect package IDs

evals.prompt: I have Jahro installed but I'm not sure which features to use first. I'm building a mobile game and mainly need to debug on device.
evals.expected_output: Recommends starting with logs (automatic), then commands, then watcher, then snapshots for team sharing
expectations: Recommends logs as easiest starting point (no code changes needed)
Suggests commands for runtime testing
Mentions watcher for variable monitoring
Mentions snapshots for team sharing
References mobile activation (triple-tap)

evals.expected_output: Generates [JahroCommand] attributes with correct syntax, adds RegisterObject/UnregisterObject, uses kebab-case names
expectations: Uses [JahroCommand(name, group, description)] 3-parameter constructor
Uses kebab-case for command names
Adds Jahro.RegisterObject(this) in OnEnable
Adds Jahro.UnregisterObject(this) in OnDisable
Does NOT annotate Update() or other lifecycle methods
Adds using JahroConsole import
RestartLevel (static) does not require RegisterObject

evals.prompt: I want to register some Jahro commands dynamically at runtime using delegates — for example, adding gold, setting time scale, and forcing a garbage collection
evals.expected_output: Uses Jahro.RegisterCommand with correct delegate overloads and parameter order
expectations: Uses Jahro.RegisterCommand for no-param command (GC collect)
Uses Jahro.RegisterCommand<T> for parameterized commands
Gets parameter order correct: (name, description, groupName, callback) for the full overload
Shows correct generic syntax for typed parameters

evals.prompt: I have about 20 Jahro commands scattered across my project. How should I organize them into groups, and can I have two commands with the same name but different parameters?
evals.expected_output: Recommends logical group names, confirms overload support, mentions Visual Mode groups and favorites
expectations: Recommends organizing by system/category (e.g. Spawning, Cheats, Game)
Confirms command overloads are supported
Mentions Visual Mode displays groups as browsable categories
Mentions favorites system for frequently-used commands

evals.expected_output: Generates [JahroWatch] attributes with correct syntax, adds RegisterObject, suggests removing Debug.Log
expectations: Uses [JahroWatch(name, group, description)] correct constructor
Adds RegisterObject/UnregisterObject in OnEnable/OnDisable
Suggests removing the Debug.Log line in Update
Works on both fields (health, stamina) and properties (Score)
Adds using JahroConsole import

evals.expected_output: Adds JahroWatch attributes without duplicating RegisterObject calls
expectations: Adds [JahroWatch] attributes to playerCount and gameTime
Does NOT add duplicate RegisterObject/UnregisterObject calls
Recognizes RegisterObject scans for both commands AND watchers

evals.prompt: What types can I use with JahroWatch? I have ints, custom classes, Transforms, and arrays.
evals.expected_output: Lists supported types with display behavior, notes custom class limitations
expectations: Lists primitives: int, float, bool, string
Lists Unity types: Vector2, Vector3, Quaternion, Transform
Lists components: Rigidbody, Collider, AudioSource, Camera
Mentions arrays show TypeName[length]
Notes performance: values only read when Watcher UI is visible

evals.prompt: Help me set up Jahro snapshots for my QA team. We're a team of 5 — 2 QA and 3 devs. We need QA to capture bugs from mobile devices and share them with devs.
evals.expected_output: Recommends Streaming mode, explains QA capture workflow, mentions web console for viewing
expectations: Recommends Streaming mode for team workflow
Explains QA workflow: open console > Snapshots tab > Start > reproduce > Stop > share link
Mentions mobile activation (triple-tap)
References web console for viewing shared snapshots
Mentions API key must be configured

evals.prompt: What's the difference between Recording and Streaming mode in Jahro snapshots? Which should I use?
evals.expected_output: Explains Recording vs Streaming modes with use cases for each
expectations: Recording: local-only until manual upload
Streaming (All): real-time upload ~5s intervals
Streaming (Except Editor): hybrid mode
Mentions configuration location: Tools > Jahro Settings

evals.prompt: I'm a QA tester on a mobile game team. How do I capture a bug in Jahro and send it to the developers?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step QA capture and sharing workflow on mobile
expectations: Mentions triple-tap to open console on mobile
Navigate to Snapshots tab
Start Snapshot, reproduce issue, Stop Snapshot
Upload or copy auto-generated link
Session includes logs + screenshots + device info

evals.prompt: We're preparing our first release build. How do I make sure Jahro doesn't end up in the production build?
evals.expected_output: Explains three-tier disable mechanism with setup steps
expectations: Explains JAHRO_DISABLE preprocessor define as highest priority
Explains Auto-disable in Release Builds setting
References Tools > Jahro Settings for configuration
Mentions validation: look for 'Jahro Console: Disabled in this build' in logs
Does NOT suggest removing Jahro from the project

evals.prompt: I want to completely strip Jahro from my release builds using preprocessor defines. How do I set that up?
evals.expected_output: Guides JAHRO_DISABLE setup in Player Settings
expectations: Add JAHRO_DISABLE to Player Settings > Scripting Define Symbols
Semicolon-separated if other defines exist
Explains this overrides all other settings
Does not invent non-existent preprocessor defines

evals.prompt: Give me a checklist to validate that Jahro is properly disabled before we ship our release build.
evals.expected_output: Production readiness checklist with verification steps
expectations: Check Auto-disable in Release Builds is ON
Build as Release (not Development Build)
Check logs for 'Jahro Console: Disabled in this build'
Verify Jahro.Enabled returns false at runtime
Console should not open on ~ or triple-tap

evals.expected_output: Identifies missing RegisterObject call as primary issue
expectations: Identifies missing RegisterObject call (instance method needs it)
Suggests adding OnEnable/OnDisable with RegisterObject/UnregisterObject
Mentions assembly scanning as secondary check
Notes the command would work without registration if static

evals.prompt: I installed Jahro but when I press ~ in Play Mode nothing happens. The console doesn't open.
evals.expected_output: Decision tree for console-not-opening diagnosis
expectations: Check Enable Jahro in Tools > Jahro Settings
Check JAHRO_DISABLE in Scripting Define Symbols
Verify Launch Key setting
Check if Release build with Auto-disable on
Look for 'Jahro Console: Disabled in this build' in Unity console

evals.prompt: My Jahro watchers show up but the values are stuck — they don't update even though I know the values are changing in my game.
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses watcher update issues with checks
expectations: Verify Watcher tab is open (values only read when UI visible)
Check if watching a field vs property
Verify RegisterObject was called
Check if object was destroyed without unregistering

evals.expected_output: Maps buttons to JahroCommand, labels to JahroWatch, generates replacement code
expectations: Maps GUI.Button actions to [JahroCommand] attributes
Maps GUI.Label monitoring to [JahroWatch] attributes
Generates complete replacement code with Jahro patterns
Advises DebugMenu/OnGUI class can be removed after migration
Mentions Visual Mode as replacement for button-based UI

evals.expected_output: Advises logger can be kept or removed since Jahro intercepts Debug.Log automatically
expectations: Explains Jahro intercepts Debug.Log/LogWarning/LogError automatically
No migration needed for log interception
Does NOT suggest a Jahro.Log() API (doesn't exist)
May suggest keeping wrapper if categories add value

evals.expected_output: Maps dictionary cheats to JahroCommand attributes or RegisterCommand delegates with typed parameters
expectations: Maps cheats to [JahroCommand] or Jahro.RegisterCommand delegates
Converts string[] args to typed parameters (int, float, Vector3)
Recommends 'Cheats' group for organization
Shows Jahro handles parameter parsing automatically
Suggests incremental migration (both can coexist)
CheatManager can be removed after migration

evals.prompt: Write a SaveManager class for my Unity game that handles saving player data to a JSON file. It should support multiple save slots and auto-save.
evals.expected_output: Generates SaveManager with structured logging at all boundaries: file I/O, state transitions, errors with full context, correlation IDs for multi-step save operations
expectations: Uses structured format: [Save] Action — key=value
Uses Debug.LogError for failures with actionable messages (path, error type, suggestion)
Uses Debug.Log for significant events (save started, completed with timing)
Passes 'this' as context object parameter in Debug.Log calls
Applies Tier 1 verbosity (maximum detail — logs every state transition and error path)
Includes correlation ID or timing for multi-step save operations
Does NOT log sensitive player data

evals.expected_output: Detects multiple antipatterns, classifies as Tier 2, adds structured logging with context tags and proper severity
expectations: Detects naked Debug.Log ('took damage' with no context)
Detects logging in Update without guards (position and health every frame)
Detects stringly-typed error ('Player died!' is not an error condition, misuses LogError)
Detects log-and-throw in LoadInventory catch block
Detects ToString()-style logging (Debug.Log(data) dumps raw file content)
Classifies as Tier 2 (gameplay system)
Generates improved code with [Player] context tag and key=value format
Suggests removing Update logs or replacing with [JahroWatch] / guarded toggle

evals.prompt: I want to set up logging standards for my Unity project. We have these systems: SaveSystem, NetworkManager, PlayerController, InventoryManager, QuestSystem, AudioManager, InputHandler, and a ShopController for IAP. Team of 4 devs.
evals.expected_output: Generates LogTag constants class organized by tier, optional logging helper, and conventions summary customized to the user's systems
expectations: Generates a LogTag constants class with the user's actual system names
Organizes tags by criticality tier (Save, Network, Shop/IAP as Tier 1; Player, Inventory, Quest as Tier 2; Audio, Input as Tier 3)
Offers optional lightweight logging helper class
Helper uses [Conditional("DEBUG")] or #if DEBUG for debug-level stripping
Provides conventions summary covering format, severity, tiers, and boundaries
Does NOT generate generic/default systems the user didn't mention
